taxonomy: Epidendrum heterothoneum is classified as a species of orchid within the family Orchidaceae. It is a member of the genus Epidendrum, which includes over 1,500 species of epiphytic orchids. The genus Epidendrum is further grouped within the subfamily Epidendroideae, one of the largest subfamilies of orchids. This classification places Epidendrum heterothoneum within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The precise classification of Epidendrum heterothoneum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Epidendroideae, Tribe: Epidendreae, Genus: Epidendrum, Species: E. heterothoneum.

intro: Epidendrum heterothoneum, also known as the "Tiger Orchid," is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities that can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum heterothoneum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin infections.
